description: A vertically-formatted designed graphic card from the Department of Labor. The upper two-thirds is a dramatic, desaturated black-and-white photograph of President Trump standing in a dark suit with a red tie and an American-flag lapel pin, arms at his sides, glowering directly at the camera; behind him is a blurred aircraft (an Air Force One-style jet) on a runway, lending a somber, powerful, almost cinematic mood. A dark navy-to-black gradient vignette frames the edges. Overlaid in large bold white serif capitals is the headline 'ENDING MINNESOTA FRAUD.' Below sits a white rounded rectangle text box bearing the logo of 'THE DAILY SIGNAL' (a conservative news outlet, with its circular 'S' mark) and quoted reporting in black serif text: 'The Department of Labor launched an investigation into Minnesota's Unemployment Insurance Program. The Labor Department sent a letter to the Minnesota Department of Employment and Economic Development saying that recent reports of fraud, waste, and abuse may compromise the integrity of its Unemployment Insurance Program, which provides temporary financial aid to workers who lose their jobs.' A small DOL/eagle seal appears at the bottom center. The propaganda technique combines a heroic, authoritative portrait of the President with citation of a sympathetic media outlet to frame an enforcement action as decisively cracking down on fraud; the accompanying tweet text explicitly ties this to 'welfare fraud being committed by TPS Somalis in Minnesota,' targeting an immigrant community. Intended for supporters of the administration's anti-fraud and immigration-restriction messaging.
notable_text: ENDING MINNESOTA FRAUD. THE DAILY SIGNAL — The Department of Labor launched an investigation into Minnesota's Unemployment Insurance Program.
